blog.learnMore: Learn more about Emergentally’s work in supporting governments, multilaterals and CSOs to address their development challenges.

hero.headline: Supporting {emphasis} to address their development challenges.
hero.lede: Emergentally is a small team of economists, PFM specialists and engineers embedded with ministries, development agencies and CSOs from Liberia and Nigeria to Côte d’Ivoire and Ethiopia. We provide software and advisory services.

dashboard.headline: The world’s simplest, most powerful development management system.
dashboard.badgeBody: The only country system to auto-update from donor IATI feeds on an ongoing basis.

features.title: Automatic donor data
features.body: Live IATI integration keeps financial data current — the only country system in the world currently doing this.

features.title: Built for PFM
features.body: IFMIS import, budget-format export, counterpart funding capture.

features.title: Manage for results
features.body: Capture outputs, outcomes and impact; flag low-disbursement projects; monitor national development plan implementation.

features.title: Fast for civil servants and technicians
features.body: Lightweight, user-friendly UI built with the people doing the work.

features.title: NGO reporting
features.body: Integrated NGO registration, accreditation and reporting.

features.title: Meets sector-specific needs
features.body: Integrates with DHIS2 and visualises sector strategic plans.

pullQuote.text: The Dashboard has allowed us to shift the conversation with partners from inputs to alignment.
pullQuote.attribution: — Henry Asor, Nigeria’s Federal Ministry of Budget and Economic Planning

services.headline: Two approaches, designed to reinforce each other.
services.lede: We pair shipped software with embedded advisory work. What we learn on the ground shapes the Dashboard; what we ship in the Dashboard sharpens our advice. Engagements range from one-off assessments and feasibility studies to multi-year support.

items.title: Development Dashboard
items.body: Production aid & public investment management software, with country-specific configuration and live IATI auto-update.

items.title: Advisory services
items.body: Embedded support for finance ministries, development partners and CSOs.

items.title: Capacity building
items.body: Workshops & training so systems and data stay useful over the long term.

where.headline: Long engagements with deep context.
where.lede: Our team is based in Africa and Europe. Our work spans Anglophone and Francophone contexts, fragile states, and rapidly-growing middle-income economies, and is always driven by the institutions we work with.

cta.body: We’ll prepare a free demonstration using IATI data for your country — no commitment, no installation. About 45 minutes.
cta.teamTitle: You’ll meet a small, friendly team.
cta.teamBody: Economists, PFM specialists and engineers — no account managers in the middle. Berlin & Nairobi.

hero.headline: Development Dashboard.
hero.lede: The world's simplest, most powerful development management system — designed with civil servants, used by ministries and agencies to manage aid and public investment.
hero.badgeWorldFirstBody: The only system in the world that automatically updates financial data from donors via IATI.
hero.badgeUsersBody: Liberia’s Ministry of Finance and Development Planning, Nigeria’s Federal Ministry of Budget and Economic Planning, and more.

items.title: Development Cooperation Dashboard
items.subtitle: Federal Ministry of Budget & Economic Planning

narrative.kicker: From Liberia, developed with civil servants
narrative.headline: Built with and for the experts.
paragraphs: Originally working with Liberia's Ministry of Finance and Development Planning, we developed the Liberia Development Dashboard — an innovative system which transforms the management of aid and public investment in the country. The system is now also used by Nigeria's Federal Ministry of Budget and Economic Planning.

The Dashboard is currently the only system in the world which automatically updates financial data from donors — significantly increasing data quality and the sustainability of the system. It's lightweight and user friendly, but has a wide range of features to meet the needs of emerging economies.

pillars.headline: Designed around the needs of each context.
pillars.lede: Each pillar exists because a real civil servant or policy-maker asked for it. Together they're why the Dashboard is fast to adopt, easy to maintain, and hard to beat.

items.title: Developed with civil servants and policy-makers.
items.body: A fast and user-friendly interface helps civil servants and technicians keep data up to date, providing the information and insights senior policy-makers need to make informed decisions.
items.caption: Implementers · USAID view

items.title: Integrated with PFM processes.
items.body: Financial data for government-funded projects can be updated by uploading from IFMIS. Data can be exported in the format needed for the budget process. We're working towards making the data useful for debt management.
items.caption: States & counties view · Nigeria

items.title: Monitoring and improving project performance.
items.body: Advanced features allow a wide variety of results information to be captured in the Dashboard. Projects with low disbursement rates can be highlighted to indicate which projects need attention to improve performance. Progress on the national development plan can be tracked through an integrated approach.
items.caption: Results & performance view

items.title: Automatically updating financial data.
items.body: For donor-financed projects, the world's most advanced IATI integration makes it possible to keep financial data up to date automatically — substantially improving data quality and reducing the burden of data collection.
items.caption: Auto-updated donor data · Liberia

items.title: Always improving
items.body: We integrate our learning from each context into the core product on an ongoing basis, to deliver improvements and new features which are useful across implementations - but customised to the needs of each context.

pullQuote.text: The Dashboard has allowed us to shift the conversation with partners from inputs to alignment.
pullQuote.attribution: — Henry Asor, Nigeria’s Federal Ministry of Budget and Economic Planning

features.headline: Everything else a ministry or agency needs.
features.lede: A focused product with a deliberately wide surface area. Below: the things that don't make the highlights reel but make the system usable every day.

items.title: IFMIS integration
items.body: Upload data from IFMIS, and download in the format required for the budget process.

items.title: Counterpart funding
items.body: Capture government counterpart funding commitments alongside donor flows.

items.title: Outputs, outcomes, impact
items.body: Data collection and visualisation for results information at every level.

items.title: Slice by anything
items.body: By funder, location (state, county), sector, aligned ministry, or national development plan pillar.

items.title: Performance reporting
items.body: Collect and visualise project performance, disbursement rates, and alignment to the national development plan.

items.title: Export, import, and interoperability
items.body: Export to Excel for summary or pivot analysis. Import via spreadsheet templates. APIs for deeper integration.

demoCta.headline: See the Dashboard with {emphasis}.
demoCta.body: Get in touch to arrange a free demonstration. If you wish, we can show you how the Dashboard would look using IATI data for your country.
